top_stocks.description:
• Alamos Gold Inc. (NYSE: AGI) is a precious-metals miner focused primarily on gold, with operations in Canada and Mexico.
• Market cap/size classification: Based on its typical exchange-traded profile among U.S.-listed gold producers, AGI is generally viewed as a mid-cap mining company (not a mega-cap like the largest diversified miners).
• Competitive advantage/position: AGI’s position is supported by producing assets and a track record of operational execution (notably at producing districts in Canada and Mexico), which can help stabilize cash generation through the gold price cycle.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-02-18: Alamos Gold reported fourth-quarter and year-end 2025 results, including net earnings of $434.9 million for Q4 2025 (or $1.03 per share).
• 2026-02-18: In the same earnings release, management announced a 60% increase in its dividend.
• 2026-02-19: Alamos Gold announced a refiling of its 2025 financial statements due to an administrative error.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=22.99 and PEG=0.34 suggest the stock is priced at a moderate earnings multiple while the growth ratio appears relatively attractive; for gold miners, this can be reasonable if margins and cash flows normalize with gold prices and cost discipline.
• Profitability: ROE=0.2206% and Profit Margin≈0.4897% are not “high” in absolute terms; however, for miners these can look compressed due to capital intensity, commodity-driven earnings variability, and depreciation/amortization effects—so the margin strength is mixed rather than clearly strong.
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=0.3982% indicates relatively modest long-term growth versus the market’s expectations for higher-multiple tech/AI firms; it’s a cautious growth profile.
• Quality: Investor Score=100/100 signals very strong screen-based quality/conviction; given the relatively reasonable valuation metrics and acceptable ROIC (ROIC=19.02%), the selection looks consistent with a value-at-reasonable-price style rather than a high-growth compounder.

top_stocks.description:
• Broadcom Inc. (NASDAQ: AVGO) is a semiconductor and infrastructure software company, with major positions across custom AI silicon and networking hardware, plus enterprise software.
• Market cap/size classification: Broadcom is commonly classified as a large-cap / mega-cap technology company by market capitalization.
• Competitive advantage/position: Broadcom’s advantage is its integrated approach—combining silicon (including custom accelerators and networking) with enterprise software and large-scale customer relationships—plus its ability to execute large AI infrastructure supply programs.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-03-04: Broadcom reported first quarter fiscal 2026 financial results and a quarterly dividend, highlighting strong AI-driven demand.
• 2026-03-04: In that release, Broadcom cited Q1 AI revenue of $8.4 billion growing 106% year-over-year.
• 2026-04-07: Coverage noted Broadcom’s multi-year TPU/AI supply visibility deals as a continuing strategic driver for forward expectations.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=74.08 with PEG=0.44 implies a high earnings multiple but relatively favorable growth-adjusted valuation; this can be reasonable if AI-related revenue durability and margins hold up.
• Profitability: ROE=0.3337% and Profit Margin≈0.3657% are lower than what many investors expect from “high-return” compounders, but the screen’s ROIC=17.38% suggests capital efficiency that may offset lower headline ROE.
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=0.4838% is modest in percentage terms; for a stock with a premium P/E, that makes the multiple highly sensitive to sentiment and near-term execution (the screen likely reflects steadier earnings power rather than explosive long-run EPS growth).
• Quality: Investor Score=100/100 supports the idea that, despite the premium valuation, the business profile and execution risk appear screened as very strong.

top_stocks.description:
• NVIDIA Corporation (NASDAQ: NVDA) designs GPUs and accelerated computing platforms used heavily for AI training/inference, data center networking, and related software ecosystems.
• Market cap/size classification: NVIDIA is commonly classified as a mega-cap technology company.
• Competitive advantage/position: NVIDIA’s advantage is its CUDA/software platform plus tight integration of hardware and system-level acceleration, which has made it a default choice in many AI data center deployments.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-02-25: NVIDIA reported fiscal fourth-quarter results (quarter ended 2026-01-31), with GAAP revenue of $68.1 billion (up 73% year-over-year) and net income around $43.0 billion.
• 2026-02-25: Reporting also highlighted that NVIDIA provided forecasts exceeding analyst expectations amid strong AI demand.
• 2026-02-25: News coverage emphasized growth and margin strength tied to the company’s AI platform leadership.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=38.62 with PEG=0.44 suggests the market is pricing in strong growth but the growth-adjusted valuation looks less extreme than many “hot” multiples; still, NVDA remains sensitive to AI capex cycles.
• Profitability: ROIC=71.75% and Profit Margin=0.556% (as provided by your screener) indicate very strong capital efficiency on a screen basis; NVDA’s business model is typically associated with high margins/returns.
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=0.3886% is modest relative to how the market often narrates NVDA’s runway; therefore, the stock’s valuation likely relies on continued multi-year AI platform demand and possibly share/earnings power beyond what the simplified EPS CAGR suggests.
• Quality: Investor Score=100/100 plus very high ROIC supports a “high-quality franchise” conclusion; overall, selection looks aligned with quality + reasonable (for NVDA) growth-adjusted valuation.

top_stocks.description:
• Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) manufactures memory and storage products, including DRAM and NAND flash, used across PCs, mobile devices, data centers, and increasingly AI infrastructure.
• Market cap/size classification: Micron is typically classified as a mid-to-large cap semiconductor company.
• Competitive advantage/position: Its advantage is technology execution in memory processes and strong exposure to AI-driven memory demand trends (including high-bandwidth memory and related enterprise/data center workloads).
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-03-18: Micron reported results for its most recent fiscal quarter referenced in market coverage, with follow-on market reactions describing strong fundamentals tied to AI demand.
• 2026-03-19 to 2026-03-30 window: Coverage reported Micron declared a quarterly dividend of $0.15 per share, with record date noted as 2026-03-30.
• 2026-04-15: The announced dividend payment date referenced across coverage is 2026-04-15 for the $0.15 quarterly dividend.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=20.14 with extremely low PEG=0.04 suggests the screen views MU as undervalued relative to its growth expectations; this is particularly attractive if earnings power is transitioning upward from a cyclical trough.
• Profitability: ROIC=29.16% and Profit Margin≈0.4149% (as provided) point to solid capital efficiency and profitability for a cyclical semiconductor manufacturer.
• Growth: EPS growth is highly front-loaded in your inputs (This Y=5.8458%) with Next Y=0.6459% and Next 5Y=1.119%; that profile implies near-term rebound/acceleration with more tempered long-run growth.
• Quality: Investor Score=100/100 and strong ROIC support the idea that MU’s current cycle positioning is being treated as high-quality rather than purely speculative; among your list, MU looks most “screen-cheap” on PEG.

top_stocks.description:
• Centerra Gold Inc. (NYSE: CGAU) is a gold-focused producer with operations in Canada and other geographic interests tied to gold production.
• Market cap/size classification: CGAU is generally viewed as a mid-cap mining company.
• Competitive advantage/position: Its position is tied to producing assets and cost structure management; in gold mining, the key differentiators are AISC discipline, reserve/production visibility, and operational reliability through the commodity cycle.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-02-19: Centerra Gold reported fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results and issued 2026 guidance.
• 2026-02-19: The company’s release reported full-year 2025 consolidated gold production costs of $1,297 per ounce and AISC (by-product basis) of $1,614 per ounce.
• 2026-02-19: The guidance/outlook was described as remaining strong as Centerra executes a self-funded growth strategy.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=6.88 with PEG=0.44 indicates very low earnings valuation on the surface; this can be attractive, but gold miners can trade cheaply when investors discount growth durability or expect margin/cost pressure.
• Profitability: ROIC=28.20% is strong on the screen, while ROE=0.3143% and Profit Margin≈0.4469% are modest; this combination can occur in mining due to earnings cyclicality and balance-sheet structure.
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=0.2113% suggests subdued long-term EPS growth; therefore, the valuation case likely depends more on commodity-price sensitivity and cost discipline than on aggressive organic growth.
• Quality: Investor Score=95/100 is high and supports the selection despite the low P/E; overall, CGAU looks like a “value/mining quality” pick where returns come from cash generation and cost management rather than high long-run growth.
